summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several recent developments in small UAS activity. Ukrainian FPV drones have struck Russian logistics targets 50 km behind the front line in Donetsk and targeted Leopard 1A5 tanks using Rubikon FPV drones. A Ukrainian firm has developed the "Chipa" net gun to physically intercept FPV drones when other defenses fail. Russia is rushing to expand its drone forces to 101,000 troops by April, while Ukraine reported hitting 105,200 targets in February. In the U.S., the Army National Guard and Tennessee Guard have purchased small FPV strike drones for training, and the Pentagon has selected a Ukrainian FPV drone from F-Drones to arm the U.S. Army. Additionally, Russian forces tested a white "penguin" suit in Ukraine, but at least two soldiers wearing it were neutralized by FPV drones within hours.
